note: The capability registry — file-as-DB. Each entry is just a tool URL + description (+ the playbook INTENTS it backs, and effect = read-only | write). Replace the urls with YOUR real tools; login is on-demand (the agent opens the url and, if a login wall shows, waits for you to log in in the browser window, then reads).

capabilities.label: ServiceNow — incident & CMDB
capabilities.description: The incident record, affected CI, and similar past incidents.

capabilities.label: Splunk — logs
capabilities.description: Error logs / stack traces and recurring error patterns around the symptom window.

capabilities.label: Grafana / Tempo — traces
capabilities.description: Distributed traces — where the latency is spent and the call dependency chain.

capabilities.label: GitHub — deploys & change history
capabilities.description: Recent deploys / commits — the 'what changed' and the exact diff.

capabilities.label: Synthetic monitor — replay
capabilities.description: Replay the user journey to confirm recovery from the user's side.

capabilities.label: Runbook — failover / rollback  (WRITE)
capabilities.description: Trigger DB failover, rollback a deploy, restart, or scale. GATED — needs human approval.

capabilities.label: PagerDuty — on-call & escalate  (WRITE)
capabilities.description: Who's on call, and page / escalate. The escalate action is GATED.
